revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安福寺 (柏原市)]]
{{Citation needed|date=October 2017|qq=}} {{Infobox Buddhist temple | Name = Anpuku-ji | | image = File:Anpukuji-Temple01.JPG | | image Caption = Main Hall | | location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka|1=Kashiwara}}, Tamatechō 7-21 | | coordinates = {{Coord|34.56291667|N|135.62930556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | | Mountain Name = Tamateyama | | Sect = {{ill|Jōdo-shū|en|Jōdo-shū|qid=Q912208|lt=Jōdo sect|1=Jōdo-shū}} | | Principal image = {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}} | | Founded = Unknown (Nara period) | | Founder = (Traditionally) {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} | | Formal Name = | | Other Names = | | Pilgrimage = | | Cultural Properties = Split bamboo-style sarcophagus lid, Sansui maki-e inkstone box, Peony maki-e inkstone box, Bodhi tree maki-e incense box (Important Cultural Properties) | | Map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|label=Anpuku-ji|float=center|relief=1|width=256|qq=}} | | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Jōdo-shū|qid=Q912208|1=Jōdo-shū}} | location = {{ill|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|1=Kashiwara}} }} '''Anpuku-ji''' (安福寺) is a {{ill|Jōdo-shū|en|Jōdo-shū|qid=Q912208|lt=Jōdo sect|1=Jōdo-shū}} {{ill|Chion-in branch|ja|知恩院派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chion-in branch|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 浄土宗|1=Jōdo-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q912208}} Buddhist temple located in Tamatechō, {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka|1=Kashiwara}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. The temple's mountain name (山号) is Tamateyama. It is situated in the central part of the Tamateyama Hills, within a valley on the northern side of {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}}. == Overview == According to temple legend, {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} founded Anpuku-ji in the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. However, by the medieval period, the temple had fallen into ruin, with only a small hall remaining. During the Kanbun era (1661–1672), a {{ill|Jōdo-shū|en|Jōdo-shū|qid=Q912208|lt=Jōdo sect|1=Jōdo-shū}} monk named Kaoku Shōnin visited the site and rebuilt the temple. {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsutomo|en|Tokugawa Mitsutomo|qid=Q843245|lt=Tokugawa Mitsutomo|1=Tokugawa Mitsutomo}}, the daimyō of Owari, deeply revered Kaoku Shōnin’s scholarship and virtue, and provided Anpuku-ji with temple lands. The temple continued to receive support from the {{ill|Owari Tokugawa family|en|Owari Tokugawa family|qid=Q841740|lt=Owari Tokugawa family|1=Owari Tokugawa family}} until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}. Among the temple's treasures, the Sansui maki-e inkstone box, Peony maki-e inkstone box, and Bodhi tree maki-e incense box, all donated by Mitsutomo, are designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan. Additionally, a split bamboo-style sarcophagus lid, discovered in the {{ill|Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|ja|玉手山古墳群|1=Tamateyama Kofun Group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Kofun Group|qq=}}, specifically from Kofun No. 3 (Shōbuyama Kofun), is displayed in the temple precincts. This lid was once repurposed as a water basin and is also designated as an Important Cultural Property. Upon passing through the temple’s first small gate, visitors encounter the '''Anpuku-ji Yokoanabo Cluster'''. These tombs are dug into exposed {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}} in the valley, with 16 confirmed on the southern side of the path and another 16 on the northern side, totaling 32 tombs. At one time, they were thought to be ancient dwellings, but the discovery of {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}, {{ill|ceramic coffins|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ceramic coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}}, and pottery—along with murals depicting mounted figures—revealed that they were {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=late Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} {{ill|cave tombs|lt=Yokoanabos|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}. The site is designated as a Historic Site by {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. After climbing the path and passing through the second gate, visitors enter the temple grounds, where a large kuri (temple kitchen and living quarters) stands immediately to the left. The main hall is located just south of this building. Directly west of the temple grounds, on a higher elevation, lies {{ill|Tamateyama Park|ja|玉手山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamateyama Park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 柏原市立玉手山公園}}, which is owned by Anpuku-ji. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National Designation) === * '''Sansui Maki-e Inkstone Box''' – Donated by {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsutomo|en|Tokugawa Mitsutomo|qid=Q843245|lt=Tokugawa Mitsutomo|1=Tokugawa Mitsutomo}} (Craftwork) – Deposited at the {{ill|Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts|en|Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts|qid=Q9004920|lt=Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts|1=Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts}}. Designated on August 1, 1899 (Meiji 32).<ref>{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|5343|Sansui Maki-e Inkstone Box – Donated by Tokugawa Mitsutomo|qq=}}</ref><ref name="list">[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture website, updated June 7, 2017), Kashiwara City section.</ref> * '''Bodhi Tree Maki-e Incense Box''' – Donated by Tokugawa Mitsutomo (Craftwork) – Deposited at the Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts. Designated on August 1, 1899.<ref>{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|5344|Bodhi Tree Maki-e Incense Box – Donated by Tokugawa Mitsutomo|qq=}}</ref><ref name="list"/> * '''Peony Maki-e Inkstone Box''' – Donated by Tokugawa Mitsutomo (Craftwork) – Deposited at the Osaka City Museum of Fine Arts. Designated on August 1, 1899.<ref>{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|5345|Peony Maki-e Inkstone Box – Donated by Tokugawa Mitsutomo|qq=}}</ref><ref name="list"/> * '''Split Bamboo-Style Sarcophagus Lid''' – Excavated from Shōbuyama Kofun in Kashiwara, Osaka (Archaeological Artifact) – Designated on June 29, 1990.<ref>{{National Cultural Properties Database|201|10071|Split Bamboo-Style Sarcophagus Lid – Excavated from Shōbuyama Kofun, Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}</ref><ref name="list"/> === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Sites''' ''' '''Anpuku-ji Yokoanabo Cluster''' – Owned by Anpuku-ji and others. Designated on March 30, 1973, with additional designations on May 1, 1984.<ref name="list"/> === Kashiwara City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Kyōcho Coffin Owned by Anpuku-ji''' (Archaeological Artifact) – Fragments of a lacquered textile coffin from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, composed of multiple layers of silk cloth and lacquer. Originally displayed in Anpuku-ji’s tokonoma (alcove), it later became recognized for its archaeological significance. The coffin, formed of 45 layers of silk, measures 94 cm in length, 47 cm in width, and 3 cm in thickness. Given the use of silk, it is believed to have belonged to a high-Ranking individual, with some speculating it could be the coffin of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} (whose grave is thought to be the {{ill|Eifuku-ji North Kofun|ja|叡福寺北古墳|1=Eifuku-ji North Kofun|2=ja|3=叡福寺北古墳|4=fr|5=Eifukuji Kita Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Eifuku-ji North Kofun|qq=}} in Taishi Town). Designated on September 23, 2011.<ref name="list"/><ref>[http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2014082200034/?doc_id=1494 Kyōcho Coffin at Anpuku-ji], [http://www.city.kashiwara.osaka.jp/docs/2016110100057/?doc_id=5647 7-4. Kyōcho Coffin at Anpuku-ji] (Kashiwara City website).</ref> == Access == * 20-minute walk west from {{ill|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|en|Kawachi-Kokubu Station|qid=Q6379456|lt=Kawachi-Kokubu Station|1=Kawachi-Kokubu Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|qid=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} * 15-minute walk east from {{ill|Dōmyōji Station|en|Dōmyōji Station|qid=Q5321260|lt=Dōmyōji Station|1=Dōmyōji Station}} on the {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|qid=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}}
== Distribution and Notable Yokoanabo Tombs National Historic Site, over 200 tombs == * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs === Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]]) === Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|qid=Q11419962|lt=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|1=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokoanabos. *
